name: Stephen Carlson
headline: Carlson is roster depth, not a fantasy bet
body:
At 29 and listed at 6-4, 248, the Princeton product sits buried on Chicago's tight end depth chart, stuck behind Colston Loveland and Cole Kmet. Expert consensus pegs him near TE116 with no meaningful ADP, and he's rostered in essentially zero leagues. His path to snaps runs entirely through injuries ahead of him; absent that, the blocking-tilted role caps any receiving upside.

The schedule offers no rescue either, with Chicago's tight ends carrying one of the league's tougher slates. Until the depth chart thins out, Carlson is a deep-bench stash at best, the kind of name you monitor only if Kmet or Loveland misses time. Leave him on waivers in standard formats and revisit only when an opportunity actually opens. There's nothing here worth a draft pick in 2026.
